’Improvement (decrease in PUCAI of less than 20 points) after 7 to 10 days, or in case of an increase in PUCAI of 20 points at any time, consider intensifying treatment. Steroid dependence must be avoided.
5	
See part 2 of these recommendations.
6	
Response is defined by a decrease in the PUCAI score of at least 20 points. However, the goal of induction therapy is complete remission in the long term.
7	
For example, in cases of prior intolerance or resistance to steroids, or when infliximab is indicated anyway as maintenance therapy after thiopurine failure.
8	
Measurement of TPMT (genotyping or enzymatic activity) at baseline, as well as levels of 6-TG and 6-MMP after 2 to 3 months, can help optimize thiopurine dosing.
9	
If infliximab was used in thiopurine-naive disease, thiopurines can be added and infliximab stopped after 4 to 8 months in case of complete remission. Switching to 5-ASA can be considered in some cases, if 5-ASA has not previously failed and after a period of sustained deep remission.
Comments: The treatments
